one_liner: Clay is a GTM data enrichment/automation platform offering an API key-based HTTP API (for triggering workflows and Clay-native enrichments) gated behind its paid Growth plan, plus an official MCP server for AI assistant integration.

auth_methods.notes: Clay API key found in Account settings; used for Clay-native integrations and as a header/bearer token for HTTP API actions. Some integrations (e.g., via Nango) also require a Workspace ID alongside the API key.

For external services requiring dynamic tokens, Clay supports an 'HTTP API with JWT Authentication' action that fetches and injects JWTs automatically based on a configured token endpoint/username/password.

self_serve_notes: A free tier exists (100 data credits, 500 actions/month) and API keys can be generated by any account holder, but the HTTP API action functionality required for external integrations is gated behind the Growth plan starting at $495/month, so full self-serve developer API access requires a paid upgrade.

api_surface.notes: Clay exposes an HTTP API action within its workflow/table builder for calling external APIs and receiving webhooks, plus native enrichment lookups via API key. It's more of an in-product automation/HTTP action surface than a fully public REST API with broad endpoint coverage.

main_blocker: Full developer HTTP API access requires the Growth plan ($495/month), which is a paid-plan gate rather than free self-serve; also the API surface is primarily oriented around Clay's internal workflow/table automation rather than a broad conventional REST API.

evidence.supports: API key found in Account settings, required for Clay-native integrations
Authentication requires API key plus Workspace ID
HTTP API with JWT authentication for dynamically generated tokens
Official MCP settings/support to connect Clay workspace to AI tools
HTTP API functionality restricted to Growth plan starting at $495/month; free tier limits

flags: API surface is workflow/action-oriented rather than a traditional standalone REST API
Paid plan required for full HTTP API access
MCP server exists both officially (@clayhq/clay-mcp) and via community project using browser session cookies (less standard auth)
